title: Week 22
summary:
Capesize
A short week due to the Spring Bank holiday ended in a flurry of fixtures for west Australia to China. Possibly over 10 vessels being concluded yesterday pushed the level from under $5.00 last week to $5.15. Of the major iron ore players Rio Tinto were said to have fixed five vessels, BHP Billiton three and FMG two vessels.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A short week due to the Spring Bank holiday ended in a flurry of fixtures for west Australia to China. Possibly over 10 vessels being concluded yesterday pushed the level from under $5.00 last week to $5.15.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The centre of attention this week has been front-haul activity from South America and with increased t/a enquiry from South America, the market looked to be holding steady. On the other hand, the north Continent remains weak with poor returns on offer.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: Some gains (albeit modest) were recorded on the US Gulf front-haul markets as reports surfaced of a nice 2014 built 61,000 dwt ultramax being fixed for a trip to China with petcoke at $13,350 daily. There was also talk as the week closed that a slightly better rate had been concluded on a similar vessel for a grain run to Japan.
